condition.selected: The scale-of-5 graph has more symbols

incorrects.description: Student selected that scale-of-5 has more symbols, opposite of correct answer

steps.text: Let's count together. Look at Blue on the right graph. Count the symbols. Now look at Blue on the left. Which column has fewer symbols?

condition.selected: They have the same number of symbols

incorrects.description: Student thinks both graphs have the same number of symbols

steps.text: Here's what helps: count the Red symbols on each graph. The left graph has many more symbols than the right, even though both show 20 items.

incorrects.description: Student thinks the graphs show different data sets

steps.text: You're working on it. Both graphs show the exact same Minis data. Red is 20, Blue is 25, Yellow is 15 on both. The difference is how many items each symbol represents.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System counts symbols in Blue column on left graph: 12.5 symbols light up one by one
System counts symbols in Blue column on right graph: 5 symbols light up one by one

steps.text: Let me show you. I'll count Blue on both. The left graph has 12 and a half symbols. The right graph has only 5 symbols. The scale-of-5 graph has fewer symbols because each one shows more items.
